contract_meta.rationale: Phase 4B integrates planning into execute flow: sequential subtask execution, progress monitoring, UI progress display. Win #36 is UI work - recommend delegation per established pattern.
contract_meta.delegation_note: Win #36 (UI progress display) recommended for friend/assistant per Phase 2B/3B pattern

project.name: executor-mvp
project.goal: Complete task planning: execute subtasks sequentially respecting dependencies, monitor progress, display in UI, estimate completion time. System can decompose and execute 'build todo app with auth' showing progress through each subtask.
project.scope: Sequential execution, progress monitoring, UI display, time estimation

delegation_recommendation.win_36_ui_work: Recommend friend handles this per established pattern
delegation_recommendation.wins_33_34_35_37: Backend work, safe for primary developer

out_of_scope: Dynamic re-planning based on failures (future enhancement)

execution_model.description: Agent verifies Phase 4A completion, integrates planning into execute flow, adds UI progress monitoring.

ui_work_protocol.if_developer_executes_36: Apply strict scope limits from Phase 2B UI protocol

observability.description: Enhanced observability for subtask execution and progress monitoring

tasks.title: Verify Phase 4A completion
tasks.description: Confirm Phase 4A planning foundation is complete before Phase 4B execution

evaluation.intent: Verify Phase 4A planning foundation complete before execution
evaluation.success_criteria: All Phase 4A artifacts exist and tests pass
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Phase 4A must be complete before Phase 4B

tasks.title: Win #33: Subtask Executor
tasks.description: Create executeSubtask.ts that executes a single subtask: generate code, test, repair if needed
tasks.work_profile: Backend orchestration combining generation, testing, repair

content_spec.function_signature: export async function executeSubtask(subtask: Subtask, context: ExecutionContext): Promise<SubtaskResult>
execution_logic: - Context from previous subtasks (what was built)
Call LLM to generate code for this subtask
Run tests (if subtask includes test generation)
Return SubtaskResult with status, files, test results, repair history

test_cases: successful subtask execution with passing tests
subtask with failing tests triggers repair
subtask failure after repair exhaustion
subtask with no tests (setup task)
subtask uses context from previous subtasks
LLM error handled gracefully
execution result structure valid

demo_requirement.manual_test: Execute individual subtasks with mocked dependencies, verify results
demo_requirement.approval_needed: Developer confirms subtask execution works correctly

tasks.title: Win #34: Sequential Orchestrator
tasks.description: Create executeTaskPlan.ts that orchestrates sequential subtask execution respecting dependencies

content_spec.function_signature: export async function executeTaskPlan(plan: TaskPlan, context: PlanExecutionContext): Promise<PlanExecutionResult>
orchestration_logic: Analyze dependencies to get execution order
- Check dependencies satisfied (all dependency subtasks completed successfully)
- If dependencies not satisfied: skip or fail based on strategy
- If subtask fails: decide whether to continue or halt (based on criticality)
Return complete execution result with all subtask results and final status

test_cases: linear plan: executes subtasks in order
plan with dependencies: respects execution order
subtask failure: continues with independent subtasks or halts based on strategy
all subtasks succeed: status 'completed'
some subtasks fail: status 'partial'
critical subtask fails early: status 'failed', remaining skipped
progress tracking updates correctly
execution result structure valid

demo_requirement.manual_test: Execute full task plan with various dependency scenarios

tasks.title: Win #35: Time Estimator
tasks.description: Create estimateCompletion.ts that estimates remaining time based on progress and historical data

content_spec.function_signature: export function estimateCompletion(progress: ProgressSnapshot, plan: TaskPlan): TimeEstimate
estimation_strategy: Calculate average time per completed subtask
Account for complexity: high complexity subtasks take 1.5x average, low take 0.75x
Add buffer based on historical repair rates (from Phase 3 metrics)
Consider critical path: longest dependency chain affects total time

test_cases: early progress: low confidence, longer estimate
mid progress: medium confidence, estimate adjusts based on actual pace
late progress: high confidence, accurate estimate
high complexity remaining: longer estimate
low complexity remaining: shorter estimate
estimate decreases as subtasks complete
confidence increases as more subtasks complete

demo_requirement.manual_test: Generate estimates at different progress points, verify reasonableness

tasks.title: Win #36: UI Progress Display
tasks.description: Add UI progress monitoring showing subtask execution, current status, estimated completion

delegation.rationale: UI work is acknowledged danger zone. Friend handled Phase 2B and 3B UI successfully.
delegation.if_developer_executes: Apply strict scope limits: functional only, no styling polish, no features beyond spec

ensure: Basic CSS for progress visualization (functional only)
Parse taskPlan and execution progress from /api/execute response
- Status icon (⏳ pending, ⚙️ in-progress, ✅ completed, ❌ failed)
Update progress bar based on completion percentage
Update UI as execution progresses (if streaming supported) or show final state
Show dependency relationships (optional: simple arrow indicators)

success_criteria: All subtasks shown with correct status

demo_requirement.manual_test: Generate projects with task plans, verify UI shows progress correctly
demo_requirement.approval_needed: Developer AND wife/friend review - confirm functional, no scope creep

forbidden: Styling beyond basic layout and colors
Real-time streaming updates (show final state only for Phase 4B)
Any features not explicitly in win spec

tasks.title: Win #37: Integrate Planning into Execute & Add Telemetry
tasks.description: Integrate task planning into /api/execute flow and add planning metrics to telemetry

ensure: Determine if prompt is complex (needs decomposition):
- Heuristic: mentions multiple features/components, or clarifications indicate complexity
- Or: always decompose and let validator decide quality
- If quality <70: execute as single task (fallback)
If not complex: execute as single task (existing flow)

test_cases: simple prompt: no decomposition, single task execution
complex prompt: decomposition, plan execution
low quality decomposition: fallback to single task
plan execution successful: all subtasks completed
plan execution partial: some subtasks failed
response includes task plan and execution results
meta file includes planning metrics
backward compatibility: existing execute behavior unchanged for simple tasks

success_criteria: Low quality decompositions fall back gracefully

demo_requirement.manual_test: POST complex prompts to /api/execute, verify planning used and progress tracked

tasks.title: Phase 4 Complete - Final Validation Gate
tasks.description: Final validation that entire Phase 4 (Task Planning & Monitoring) is complete and production-ready

actions.description: End-to-end Phase 4 validation with complex prompts
test_scenarios: Simple prompt: 'build Flask hello world' -> no decomposition, single task
Medium prompt: 'build todo app with auth' -> decompose into 5-7 subtasks, execute sequentially
Complex prompt: 'build e-commerce site' -> decompose into 8-10 subtasks, execute
Verify UI shows progress for decomposed tasks
Verify meta file includes planning metrics
Verify time estimation updates as progress continues

evaluation.intent: Confirm entire Phase 4 (Task Planning & Monitoring) is production-ready
evaluation.success_criteria: All tests pass, planning works end-to-end, UI functional, decomposition quality good, sequential execution correct
evaluation.on_pass: PHASE 4 COMPLETE - System decomposes and executes complex tasks with progress monitoring. Ready for Phase 5 or Phase 4.5 decision.
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Fix issues before declaring Phase 4 complete

phase_4_completion_metrics.decomposition_quality: Average quality score >75 for complex prompts
phase_4_completion_metrics.execution_success: Subtasks execute in correct dependency order
phase_4_completion_metrics.fallback_works: Low quality decompositions fall back to single task gracefully

win_isolation.description: Each win must be complete, tested, and DEMOED before proceeding to next
win_isolation.implementation: Run tests after each win. Manual demo required for all wins.

P4-GATE.significance: Phase 4 completion - autonomous task planning milestone
P4-GATE.on_fail: HALT execution. Fix issues before declaring Phase 4 complete.
P4-GATE.report: Generate comprehensive Phase 4 completion report with planning metrics.

delegation_protocol.win_36_ui: Recommend friend handles this per established pattern
delegation_protocol.developer_option: If developer insists on executing Win #36, apply strict Phase 2B UI scope limits

fallback_policies.phase4a_verification_fails: HALT - Phase 4A must be complete before 4B.
fallback_policies.test_fails: Fix implementation, do not skip tests.
fallback_policies.coverage_drops: Add tests to maintain >80% coverage before proceeding.
fallback_policies.decomposition_quality_poor: Fall back to single task execution, don't force planning.
fallback_policies.ui_scope_creep: Remove additions or defer to future phase. Stick to functional spec only.
fallback_policies.subtask_execution_fails: Continue with independent subtasks or halt based on criticality.

release_actions.cmd: git commit -m "feat(phase4): complete task planning and monitoring - decomposition, sequential execution, progress tracking, UI display"

post_release_celebration.rationale: Phase 4 is major autonomous capability milestone. System now decomposes complex tasks into subtasks and executes them sequentially with progress monitoring. This is where it starts feeling 'autonomous' - it plans its own work.
requirements: Full day off with wife and kids
Discuss next steps together when refreshed (not today)

decision_point.phase_5: Memory & production readiness (learning from experience, security scanning)
decision_point.phase_4_5: Production hardening (CI/CD security, SLSA provenance, SBOMs from assistant's audit)
decision_point.extended_break: Use the system for real work, validate value before continuing
decision_point.recommendation: Discuss with wife and consider external auditor feedback before deciding

ai_executor_guidelines.observability_first: Trace all decisions. Log reasoning for each win completion.
ai_executor_guidelines.win_isolation: Complete and test each win fully before proceeding to next.
ai_executor_guidelines.delegation_awareness: Win #36 is UI work. Recommend friend handles per established pattern.
ai_executor_guidelines.test_driven: Write comprehensive tests for orchestration and execution logic.
ai_executor_guidelines.fail_fast: If any test fails, halt and fix before proceeding.
ai_executor_guidelines.gate_enforcement: P4-GATE is Phase 4 completion - requires complex prompt validation.
ai_executor_guidelines.planning_quality: Decomposition quality critical - poor decompositions should fall back to single task.

governance_note.for_human: Phase 4B completes task planning and monitoring. Wins 33-35-37 are backend (safe for you). Win 36 is UI (recommend friend handles per established pattern). After P4-GATE passes, Phase 4 is COMPLETE. Major autonomous capability achieved. Take full day off. Celebrate with family. Then decide together: Phase 5 (memory/learning) OR Phase 4.5 (production hardening) OR extended break to use the system. Consider external auditor findings in decision. No pressure to continue immediately.
